documents.section: Chapter II - Obligations of Data Fiduciary
documents.content: Data Fiduciary shall obtain valid consent from Data Principal before processing personal data. Consent shall be free, specific, informed, unconditional, and unambiguous with clear affirmative action. Data Fiduciary shall provide notice of data processing including purpose, types of data collected, retention period, and rights available to Data Principal.

In case of personal data breach, Data Fiduciary shall notify the Data Protection Board and affected Data Principals within the time prescribed. The notification shall include description of breach, nature of personal data affected, potential consequences, and remedial actions taken.

Where supplier supplies goods or renders services to buyer, buyer shall make payment on or before agreed date in writing or where no agreement, before appointed day. The period agreed upon shall not exceed 45 days from day of acceptance or deemed acceptance.

Where buyer fails to make payment as required, buyer shall be liable to pay compound interest with monthly rests at three times of the bank rate notified by Reserve Bank of India, from the appointed day or date immediately following the agreed date.

documents.section: Section 393 - TDS on Payments to Residents
documents.content: TDS rates: 2% on commission/brokerage, 2% on rent (machinery/plant), 10% on rent (land/building), 1% on immovable property transfer consideration exceeding Rs. 50 lakhs, 2% on contractor payments (other than individual/HUF), 10% on professional fees.

Failure to deduct or pay TDS results in disallowance of 30% of sum payable to resident. For payments to non-residents, entire sum is disallowed. Interest at 1% per month for delay in deduction, 1.5% per month for delay in payment after deduction. Penalty equal to tax amount. Prosecution with imprisonment up to 7 years.

Supply includes all forms of supply for consideration in course of business. Schedule I includes activities without consideration (e.g., permanent transfer of assets with ITC claimed). Schedule III excludes certain activities (e.g., employee services).

Registered person supplying goods shall issue tax invoice before or at time of removal. For services, invoice within 30 days of supply. Invoice must contain GSTIN, serial number, date, description, value, tax rate and amount.

Tax invoice must contain name, address, GSTIN of supplier, consecutive serial number not exceeding 16 characters, date of issue, name and GSTIN of recipient, HSN code, description, quantity, value, taxable value, rate and amount of tax.

Return of Assets - All assets shall be returned upon termination or change of employment. Organizations shall document procedures for asset return including timelines, verification of return, and consequences of non-return.

Privacy and Protection of PII - Organizations shall identify and comply with PII protection requirements. Data processing agreements shall specify purpose, scope, nature of processing, duration, and rights and obligations of parties.

Software license agreements should specify scope of license (users, purpose, duration, territory), acceptance testing procedures, maintenance and support obligations, source code escrow arrangements, IP ownership and license grants, warranties, indemnities, liability limitations, confidentiality, and termination rights.
AES recommended for symmetric encryption. RSA and ECC recommended for asymmetric encryption. Minimum key lengths: RSA 2048, ECDSA P-256. Key exchanges must use Diffie-Hellman, IKE, or ECDH.

Standard vendor agreement provisions include: termination for cause with cure period, limitation of liability (capped at fees paid), indemnification for IP infringement, confidentiality obligations, audit rights, governing law (Washington state), and arbitration dispute resolution.

Courts interpret contracts using objective standard - words given ordinary and natural meaning. Entire agreement clauses exclude pre-contractual statements. Disclaimers exclude implied warranties. External documents incorporated by reference are binding even if not read.

Confidential Information excludes information that is or becomes publicly available, was lawfully known before disclosure, is received from third party without restriction, or is independently developed. Confidentiality obligations survive termination without time limit.

Prohibited activities include unauthorized copying of copyrighted material, installing unlicensed software, accessing data without authorization, introducing malware, revealing passwords, port scanning without notification, and interfering with network service.

Multi-Factor Authentication required for all privileged accounts. Privileged account management systems shall automatically rotate credentials using unique credentials for each system. Organizations shall log and alert changes to privileged group memberships.
